shortDescription: German Embassy · Qualified employment · Recognition of qualifications

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current job seeker visa requirements on make-it-in-germany.com. Opportunity Card may supersede traditional job seeker for many applicants—assess correct pathway.

alert.title: Qualification recognition
alert.body: Verify degree recognition (Anabin/ZAB) and job offer terms before embassy appointment.

about.label: Description
about.value: National visa allowing qualified professionals with recognised German-equivalent degree to search for employment in Germany, typically up to 6 months.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Recognised higher education qualification · Sufficient funds (blocked account) · Health insurance · CV and job search plan · May need vocational recognition for some fields

about.label: Recognition prerequisite
about.value: Degree must be recognised as equivalent to German qualification via Anabin or statement of comparability.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: German Embassy in India · ZAB for recognition · Ausländerbehörde after arrival

about.label: After approval
about.value: Enter Germany; attend interviews; convert to work permit when employment secured.

eligibility.criterion: Recognised qualification or equivalent skilled worker profile
Concrete job offer matching qualification (if required)
German language level per role requirements

redFlagsBanner: If qualification not recognised, complete ZAB recognition or assess Opportunity Card points pathway before applying.

redFlags.title: Degree not recognised
redFlags.fix: Apply for recognition; consider Opportunity Card if eligible

redFlags.title: Insufficient blocked account
redFlags.fix: Open blocked account for full required period

redFlags.title: Weak job search plan
redFlags.description: Generic plan without target industry or qualifications match.
redFlags.fix: Industry-specific search plan with target companies

redFlags.title: Wrong pathway chosen
redFlags.description: Applied job seeker when Opportunity Card more suitable.

redFlags.title: Health insurance gaps
redFlags.description: Travel insurance not valid for entire stay.
redFlags.fix: Compliant health insurance for full visa period

redFlags.title: Prior refusals hidden
redFlags.description: Schengen or German refusals not disclosed.

faqs.q: Can we guarantee job seeker visa approval?
faqs.a: No. Embassy assesses recognition, funds, and credibility. Never promise approval or employment.

faqs.q: Job seeker vs Opportunity Card?
faqs.a: Opportunity Card uses points system; traditional job seeker requires recognised degree. Assess which fits.

faqs.q: Can work while searching?
faqs.a: Generally cannot work on job seeker visa—limited exceptions. Verify current rules.

faqs.q: How long to find job?
faqs.a: Visa typically 6 months—employment must match qualification level.

faqs.q: Blocked account amount?
faqs.a: Verify current requirement—similar to student amount (~€11,904 for 2026).

faqs.q: Recognition process time?
faqs.a: ZAB statement can take weeks—start early.

faqs.q: After job offer?
faqs.a: Apply for work residence permit at Ausländerbehörde.

faqs.q: Does visa guarantee job?
faqs.a: No. Visa allows search only—employment depends on market and qualifications.

faqs.q: What is Description for this service?
faqs.a: National visa allowing qualified professionals with recognised German-equivalent degree to search for employment in Germany, typically up to 6 months.

faqs.q: What is Eligible applicants for this service?
faqs.a: Recognised higher education qualification · Sufficient funds (blocked account) · Health insurance · CV and job search plan · May need vocational recognition for some fields

faqs.q: What is Recognition prerequisite for this service?
faqs.a: Degree must be recognised as equivalent to German qualification via Anabin or statement of comparability.

faqs.q: Important note on Recognition prerequisite?
faqs.a: Degree must be recognised as equivalent to German qualification via Anabin or statement of comparability.

faqs.q: What is Key authority for this service?
faqs.a: German Embassy in India · ZAB for recognition · Ausländerbehörde after arrival

faqs.q: What is After approval for this service?
faqs.a: Enter Germany; attend interviews; convert to work permit when employment secured.

faqs.q: Is Recognised qualification (Anabin/ZAB) required?
faqs.a: Verify before apply

faqs.q: Is Blocked account or financial proof required?
faqs.a: Assess whether Blocked account or financial proof is satisfied for this case.

faqs.q: Is Health insurance for stay required?
faqs.a: Assess whether Health insurance for stay is satisfied for this case.

faqs.q: Is Detailed CV and cover letter required?
faqs.a: Detailed CV and cover letter is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Job search plan required?
faqs.a: Job search plan is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Valid passport required?
faqs.a: Valid passport is typically required.

faqs.q: Is Accommodation plan in Germany required?
faqs.a: Accommodation plan in Germany is typically required.

faqs.q: What is the Processing time?
faqs.a: 8–12w — Embassy processing

faqs.q: What is the Government fee?
faqs.a: €75 — National visa approx

faqs.q: What is the Our approval rate?
faqs.a: 79% — ~68% benchmark

faqs.q: What is the Required docs?
faqs.a: 13 — + recognition & funds

faqs.q: What is the Consultancy fee?
faqs.a: See fee tab — + govt & blocked account

faqs.q: What if the client has Degree not recognised?
faqs.a: Apply for recognition; consider Opportunity Card if eligible

faqs.q: Why is Degree not recognised a problem?
faqs.a: Qualification not equivalent on Anabin/ZAB.

faqs.q: What if the client has Insufficient blocked account?
faqs.a: Open blocked account for full required period

faqs.q: Why is Insufficient blocked account a problem?
faqs.a: Funds below required amount.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent must be on file before submission
Never guarantee visa or immigration approval; use approved-language only
Fee quotes must separate consultancy, government, and third-party costs
proTips: Compare Opportunity Card vs job seeker at intake
Research target industry employers before arrival
postApproval: Explain job search timeline and limits
Advise on converting to work permit
Connect to Make it in Germany job portal

timeline.title: ZAB if needed, blocked account, documents

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Always check if client qualifies for Opportunity Card first—may be better pathway than traditional job seeker.
